orgs.title: List organization migrations

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: exclude
parameters.description: Exclude attributes from the API response to improve performance

items.description: Allowed values that can be passed to the exclude param.

orgs.descriptionHTML:
Lists the most recent migrations, including both exports (which can be started through the REST API) and imports (which cannot be started using the REST API).

A list of repositories is only returned for export migrations.

repositories.description: The repositories included in the migration. Only returned for export migrations.

private.description: Whether the repository is private or public.

size.description: The size of the repository, in kilobytes. Size is calculated hourly. When a repository is initially created, the size is 0.

default_branch.description: The default branch of the repository.

is_template.description: Whether this repository acts as a template that can be used to generate new repositories.

pull_request_creation_policy.description: The policy controlling who can create pull requests: all or collaborators_only.

disabled.description: Returns whether or not this repository disabled.

visibility.description: The repository visibility: public, private, or internal.

allow_rebase_merge.description: Whether to allow rebase merges for pull requests.

allow_squash_merge.description: Whether to allow squash merges for pull requests.

allow_auto_merge.description: Whether to allow Auto-merge to be used on pull requests.

delete_branch_on_merge.description: Whether to delete head branches when pull requests are merged

allow_update_branch.description: Whether or not a pull request head branch that is behind its base branch can always be updated even if it is not required to be up to date before merging.

use_squash_pr_title_as_default.description: Whether a squash merge commit can use the pull request title as default. **This property is closing down. Please use `squash_merge_commit_title` instead.

squash_merge_commit_title.description:
The default value for a squash merge commit title:

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE` - default to the commit's title (if only one commit) or the pull request's title (when more than one commit).

squash_merge_commit_message.description:
The default value for a squash merge commit message:

- `PR_BODY` - default to the pull request's body.
- `COMMIT_MESSAGES` - default to the branch's commit messages.
- `BLANK` - default to a blank commit message.

merge_commit_title.description:
The default value for a merge commit title.

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `MERGE_MESSAGE` - default to the classic title for a merge message (e.g., Merge pull request #123 from branch-name).

merge_commit_message.description:
The default value for a merge commit message.

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `PR_BODY` - default to the pull request's body.
- `BLANK` - default to a blank commit message.

allow_merge_commit.description: Whether to allow merge commits for pull requests.

allow_forking.description: Whether to allow forking this repo

web_commit_signoff_required.description: Whether to require contributors to sign off on web-based commits

anonymous_access_enabled.description: Whether anonymous git access is enabled for this repository

code_search_index_status.description: The status of the code search index for this repository

exclude.description: Exclude related items from being returned in the response in order to improve performance of the request. The array can include any of: `"repositories"`.

items.description: Allowed values that can be passed to the exclude parameter. The array can include any of: `"repositories"`.

orgs.title: Start an organization migration

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: repositories
bodyParameters.description: A list of arrays indicating which repositories should be migrated.

bodyParameters.name: lock_repositories
bodyParameters.description: Indicates whether repositories should be locked (to prevent manipulation) while migrating data.

bodyParameters.name: exclude_metadata
bodyParameters.description: Indicates whether metadata should be excluded and only git source should be included for the migration.

bodyParameters.name: exclude_git_data
bodyParameters.description: Indicates whether the repository git data should be excluded from the migration.

bodyParameters.name: exclude_attachments
bodyParameters.description: Indicates whether attachments should be excluded from the migration (to reduce migration archive file size).

bodyParameters.name: exclude_releases
bodyParameters.description: Indicates whether releases should be excluded from the migration (to reduce migration archive file size).

bodyParameters.name: exclude_owner_projects
bodyParameters.description: Indicates whether projects owned by the organization or users should be excluded. from the migration.

bodyParameters.name: org_metadata_only
bodyParameters.description: Indicates whether this should only include organization metadata (repositories array should be empty and will ignore other flags).

bodyParameters.name: exclude
bodyParameters.description: Exclude related items from being returned in the response in order to improve performance of the request. Supported values are: repositories

orgs.descriptionHTML:
Initiates the generation of a migration archive.

Before you can use this endpoint, you must configure a blob storage provider in the "Migrations" section in the Management Console. For more details, see "Migrating repositories from GitHub Enterprise Server to GitHub Enterprise Cloud."

allow_forking.description: Whether to allow forking this repo

web_commit_signoff_required.description: Whether to require contributors to sign off on web-based commits

orgs.title: Get an organization migration status

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: migration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the migration.

parameters.name: exclude
parameters.description: Exclude attributes from the API response to improve performance

items.description: Allowed values that can be passed to the exclude param.

orgs.descriptionHTML:
Fetches the status of a migration.

The state of a migration can be one of the following values:

  • pending, which means the migration hasn't started yet.
  • exporting, which means the migration is in progress.
  • exported, which means the migration finished successfully.
  • failed, which means the migration failed.

response.description:
• pending, which means the migration hasn't started yet.
  • exporting, which means the migration is in progress.
  • exported, which means the migration finished successfully.
  • failed, which means the migration failed.

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE` - default to the commit's title (if only one commit) or the pull request's title (when more than one commit).

squash_merge_commit_message.description:
The default value for a squash merge commit message:

- `PR_BODY` - default to the pull request's body.
- `COMMIT_MESSAGES` - default to the branch's commit messages.
- `BLANK` - default to a blank commit message.

The default value for a merge commit title.

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `MERGE_MESSAGE` - default to the classic title for a merge message (e.g., Merge pull request #123 from branch-name).

merge_commit_message.description:
The default value for a merge commit message.

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `PR_BODY` - default to the pull request's body.
- `BLANK` - default to a blank commit message.

• pending, which means the migration hasn't started yet.
  • exporting, which means the migration is in progress.

orgs.title: Download an organization migration archive

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: migration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the migration.

orgs.descriptionHTML: Fetches the URL to a migration archive.

orgs.title: Delete an organization migration archive

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: migration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the migration.

orgs.descriptionHTML: Deletes a previous migration archive. Migration archives are automatically deleted after seven days.

orgs.title: Unlock an organization repository

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: migration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the migration.

parameters.name: repo_name
parameters.description: repo_name parameter

orgs.descriptionHTML: Unlocks a repository that was locked for migration. You should unlock each migrated repository and delete them when the migration is complete and you no longer need the source data.

orgs.title: List repositories in an organization migration

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: migration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the migration.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

orgs.descriptionHTML: List all the repositories for this organization migration.

size.description: The size of the repository, in kilobytes. Size is calculated hourly. When a repository is initially created, the size is 0.

pull_request_creation_policy.description: The policy controlling who can create pull requests: all or collaborators_only.

advanced_security.description:
Enable or disable GitHub Advanced Security for the repository.

For standalone Code Scanning or Secret Protection products, this parameter cannot be used.

dependabot_security_updates.description: Enable or disable Dependabot security updates for the repository.

status.description: The enablement status of Dependabot security updates for the repository.

users.title: Download a user migration archive

parameters.name: migration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the migration.

users.descriptionHTML:
Fetches the URL to download the migration archive as a tar.gz file. Depending on the resources your repository uses, the migration archive can contain JSON files with data for these objects:

  • attachments
  • bases
  • commit_comments
  • issue_comments
  • issue_events
  • issues
  • milestones
  • organizations
  • projects
  • protected_branches
  • pull_request_reviews
  • pull_requests
  • releases
  • repositories
  • review_comments
  • schema
  • users

The archive will also contain an attachments directory that includes all attachment files uploaded to GitHub.com and a repositories directory that contains the repository's Git data.
